citation: Kaushik, Hovy & Lipton (2020). Learning the Difference that Makes a Difference with Counterfactually-Augmented Data. ICLR 2020. arXiv:1909.12434
notes: CAD was published in 2019-2020 and is therefore NOT post-training-cutoff; it addresses the *shortcut/near-solved* prong of R7, not memorization. Expect materially lower accuracy - that itself diversifies the 'explanations of easy predictions' concern.
Only the REVISED (counterfactual) dev+test rows are ingested; originals are excluded (shortcut-broken text is the point, and original/revised pairs would be ~85%-overlap near-dups by design).
Vetting was RULE-BASED for this dataset (scripts/curation_vetting.py cad_imdb section), not per-instance human judgment; human vetting can amend later.
